description_long: This is a vintage, industrial-style kettle made primarily of metal, featuring a cylindrical body with a slightly domed top. The kettle has a robust, top-mounted wooden handle supported by two metal arms, allowing for easy carrying and pouring. A prominent, round, hinged lid is attached to the side, which can be opened for filling or cleaning, and a small spout is visible for pouring liquids. The surface of the kettle shows signs of wear, with a patina and some rust, giving it an aged, utilitarian appearance. There are several functional components, including a pressure valve and a knob on the lid, suggesting it may be a pressurized or steam kettle. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible decorative patterns, emphasizing function over form.
description: A vintage metal kettle with a wooden handle, hinged lid, and visible spout, showing signs of age and wear. Its utilitarian design features functional components and a patinated surface.
description_view_0: Front view showing the cylindrical body, wooden handle, hinged lid open to the side, and spout.
description_view_1: Top angled view highlighting the open lid, handle structure, and the round opening of the kettle.
description_view_2: Rear top view focusing on the handle, closed lid, and the overall cylindrical shape with minimal detail on the spout.
description_view_3: Side angled view showing the handle, pressure valve, and the closed lid with the spout partially visible.
